figures.caption: TABLE IV TEACHER NETWORK RMSE COMPARISON ON NGSIM AND HIGHD DATASETS

Fig. 1. Architecture overview of the proposed teacher–student framework for autonomous-driving policy learning. The teacher network (upper) incorporates surround-aware GATv2 encoding for inter-vehicle relationship modelling, hybrid Mamba–SWA mechanisms for spatiotemporal reasoning, and MoE-Transformer blocks for computational efficiency. The student network (lower) employs lightweight GRU–SE encoding and LoRA-parameterised policy heads for efficient knowledge transfer. Multi-level distillation enables feature alignment across intermediate representations while maintaining policy optimisation through environmental feedback.

TABLE V STUDENT NETWORK ADE/FDE COMPARISON AFTER KNOWLEDGE DISTILLATION

Fig. 2. Hybrid attention mechanism in the teacher model. The architecture processes temporal sequences through Mamba blocks with state space modeling (top), applies shift-window attention for spatial reasoning (middle), and employs mixture-of-experts routing for computational efficiency (bottom). Mathematical formulations show the core operations at each stage.
